key_insight: A large Cayley-graph census shows that many finite-group properties leave measurable geometric and spectral signatures that can be learned from graph data.
engineering_relevance: The dataset and benchmarks provide a structured testbed for graph-based models to infer algebraic properties from network representations, useful for automated discovery and classification in computational algebra.
method: Constructed a dataset of 131,406 Cayley graphs covering all groups of order ≤767 except 512, with exact algebraic labels.
Collected graph, cycle, distance, and spectral statistics to study how group structure is reflected in graph observables.
Compared classical models, an MLP, and GNNs (notably GIN and sometimes GCN) for property prediction.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports that engineered graph statistics are highly informative, and that GNNs recover substantial structural signal, with evaluation across the full Cayley-graph census.
watch_item: Results may depend on group order and feature design, so generalization beyond the enumerated orders and graph families should be validated carefully.
paper_title: Learning the Graphical Nature of Symmetries
